summary: Iteration 77 - All 7 test scenarios passed. Verified: (1) Admin create member without level_id defaults to level_free and cms_roles=['role_member']. (2) Admin create with explicit level_id keeps that level. (3) Friendlier no-enrollment message - does NOT contain 'did not go through', DOES contain 'created directly from the CMS'. (4) Backend dict-value coercion works: dict→comma-joined truthy keys, list→comma-joined, bool→'Yes'/'No', int→string. (5) Frontend renders enrollment modal without React crash when dict values present. (6) Free-level member sidebar only shows 'Membership Profile'. (7) Regression tests from iteration 75/76 pass.
critical_code_review_comments: membership.py admin_create_member correctly defaults level_id to lowest-order level (level_free) when not specified
membership.py admin_create_member correctly sets cms_roles=['role_member'] for new members
enrollment.py _flatten() helper correctly coerces dict/list/bool/int to strings
MembersManager.js no-enrollment message updated to friendlier wording
MembersManager.js has defensive object→string coercion fallback in enrollment answer render
seed_data_creation: Temporary test enrollment with dict/list/bool/int values was created and cleaned up after testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 77 verified all 7 bug fixes: (1) Admin create member defaults to level_free when level_id not specified. (2) Explicit level_id is preserved. (3) No-enrollment message is friendlier. (4) Backend coerces dict/list/bool/int to strings. (5) Frontend doesn't crash with dict enrollment values. (6) Free-level members only see Membership Profile in sidebar. (7) All regression tests pass.
